body_txt: A.K.A. "The Draft". In a nutshell the word "conscription" refers to forced service in one's country's armed forces or civil services, on pain of imprisonment or (worst case scenario) death. Nations both real and fictional enact campaigns of conscription for a variety of reasons. Maybe they are a small nation overwhelmed by a larger opponent. Maybe a war of attrition has left their forces decimated and badly in need of additional manpower. Maybe making it really easy to opt into alternative non-military service is cheaper and easier (for the government) than hiring highway clean-up crews and hospital orderlies on the open job market. In many countries the conscription serves as a Rite of Passage : a man is not considered to be a man until he has served his conscription tour of duty . Of course, conscription can also be a tyrant's method of breaking the will of his subjects and fostering blind obedience. Another reason for conscription is to foster a sense of national solidarity; everyone will have the same experience of serving in the armed forces though this sometimes backfires with many critics (especially Libertarians, people with individualistic views, and people who are skeptical of the government) believing conscription is a form of slavery or involuntary servitude which goes against many freedoms and rights, causing national division of the idea of conscription; even with conscription, a nation can still experience social fragmentation. Whatever the reason, conscription has a long history in both fiction and the real world. But conscription is a double-edged sword. Conscripted armies are typically (though not always) less disciplined and motivated than professional ones, particularly if you only draw from the lower classes of society while allowing the rich , powerful, and well-connected to walk away scot-free . There's also the risk of your conscripts going From Camouflage to Criminal once the war's over... after all, you did just give them not only military training, but a grudge against the state. In cases of emergency, Lowered Recruiting Standards can occur. People with medical conditions or mental health issues, or young teens, or older people (50+) may get drafted. Or perhaps the military will offer to release prisoners if they serve on the front . Conscription is the Trope Codifier for Cannon Fodder and Men Are the Expendable Gender , and the Trope Maker for Slave Mooks . Draft Dodging is when someone who has been conscripted seeks to get out of it somehow (by eluding recruitment, going into exile, or having wealthy parents pull some strings). See also Press-Ganged (for when this is done using brute force) and Got Volunteered (for when it might as well be). The Mutant Draft Board oversees a Speculative Fiction variant where conscription is applied exclusively to Differently Powered Individuals . Also see Pre-War Civilian Career for what the conscriptee did before their military service. Not to be confused with Drafting Mechanic .
